Reading the Pro · With and without the read

Same question. Same facts. One difference.

Rosa Kim · Painter · Chicago — skeptical (Trust)

You've met Rosa. She's skeptical — burned by a lead platform before. Here's one discovery moment, handled by two AEs. Same question from Rosa, same facts on the table. Only one of them reads the signal.

What Rosa asks — both times
"I've been burned before. Why is this different?"
Rosa
↓  Two AEs answer the exact same question  ↓
Without the read
Not yet played

Rosa"I've been burned before. Why is this different?"

AE"Totally hear you. A lot of pros love us. I really think you'll see a difference — we've got a great platform and a lot of happy customers, so I'd just say give it a shot."

Rosa"...Right. That's kind of what the last one said too."

→Rosa stays unmoved. Generic reassurance sounds exactly like the platform that burned her.
With the read
Not yet played

Rosa"I've been burned before. Why is this different?"

AE"Fair — and you should be skeptical. So let me be specific instead of selling you. You told me the last platform sent you tire-kickers. Here's exactly how lead targeting works in your trade and your area, and here's what you'd actually be able to control about who reaches you. If that doesn't hold up, you'll know fast."

Rosa"Okay. That's more like it. Keep going."

→Rosa leans in. Named her real experience, got concrete, invited scrutiny.
🎧Listen to both versions to continue.
Make the call
Same question, same facts. What made the second AE land with Rosa?

What changed — and what didn't

→ Rosa isn't disengaged — she's skeptical. She's still in the conversation because she wants to be convinced. Read that, and you lead with evidence, not enthusiasm.
Without the read

Pitched warmth — "a lot of pros love us."

Generic, evidence-light, treated her like any Pro.

Sounded like the platform that already burned her.

With the read

Led with specifics — targeting, control, her trade and area.

Named her actual bad experience instead of talking past it.

Invited scrutiny: "If that doesn't hold up, you'll know fast."

Same question. Same facts on the table. No pricing, no packages — the only variable was whether the AE read the signal and adjusted how they answered.
Reading the Pro · With and without the read

Watch the read do its work.

You've been naming signals. Now see what reading one actually changes on a call.

Same Pro, same question, two AEs. Listen to both, then decide what made the difference.

With and without the read · complete

The read changes the delivery, not the facts.

  • Skeptical is engagement, not resistance. Rosa pushes back because she's still deciding. Lead with evidence and specifics — she wants to be convinced.
  • Same facts, different framing. Both AEs had the same thing to offer. Only one read the signal and adjusted how they said it.
  • Warmth isn't a read. Reassurance at a skeptical Pro sounds like every platform that let her down. Specificity is what earns the next sentence.
